findings.title: Deduped fetch failure propagates to all concurrent bot coroutines simultaneously
findings.description: When multiple bot coroutines dedup onto the same Future via return await existing and _fetch raises (e.g. network down, yfinance error), every bot waiting on that Future receives the same exception at the same tick. Each bot catch block logs WARN and returns (skipping the tick) which is safe. However the blast radius from dedup means all bots fail together rather than independently — a new behavior introduced by F26. If _fetch raises on the Alpaca one-shot retry inside shared.py line 456, a high-bot-count scenario amplifies a single transient network error into all bots simultaneously missing a tick.
findings.evidence: backend/shared.py:482-493 — secondary waiters call return await existing with no independent error handling. bot_runner.py:233-238 wraps the call in try/except (correct), but all deduped callers hit that except branch simultaneously.
findings.recommendation: Document the shared-fate behavior in the fetch_ohlcv_async docstring. Optionally: on future failure, pop the key immediately before re-raising so subsequent calls retry independently rather than all seeing a completed-failed future.

findings.title: BatchQuickBacktestRequest.symbols has no length cap — unbounded blocking loop on sync route
findings.description: The batch backtest endpoint runs sequentially with no timeout, no max-symbols guard, and each iteration calls _fetch (a blocking network call) on the calling thread. FastAPI sync routes run in a thread pool; a request with hundreds of symbols monopolises a worker thread for minutes, potentially exhausting the pool and causing health checks to time out. The PR adds Field(gt=0) constraints to numerics but leaves symbols: list[str] fully unconstrained. Pre-existing issue but this validation hardening PR is the natural place to add it.
findings.evidence: backend/routes/backtest_quick.py:55 symbols: list[str] — no max_length or validator. Lines 207-228: tight sequential loop, no early-exit or cap.
findings.recommendation: Add a @field_validator(symbols) that raises ValueError if len(v) > 100. Pydantic v2 also supports Field(..., max_length=100) on list types directly.

findings.title: Completion-race: done future seen, new fetch created before TTL cache is populated
findings.description: The dedup check not existing.done() correctly skips a running future. But between the future completing (done=True) and the owner finally popping the key, a new coroutine can see existing.done()==True, skip it, and dispatch a duplicate run_in_executor. This is a sub-microsecond cooperative-scheduling window and is benign in practice because any duplicate fetch will hit the TTL cache immediately. The module comment claims atomicity via no-await-between-check-and-store, which is correct for the creation path but does not cover the completion-race on the read path.
findings.evidence: backend/shared.py:482-484 — done() is True immediately when executor finishes but before finally pops the key.
findings.recommendation: No code change required. Add a comment noting the completion-race is benign because the TTL cache absorbs the redundant fetch.

residual_risks.description: fetch_ohlcv_async atomicity relies on single-threaded asyncio event loop. With uvicorn workers > 1 (multi-process), the module-level _async_ohlcv_futures dict is per-process so dedup still works within a process but not across processes. Not a bug at current deployment scale but worth noting.

The Alpaca one-shot retry in _fetch (shared.py lines 455-458) has no backoff — pre-existing, not introduced by this PR. In a deduped scenario where the retry also fails, all waiting bots receive the exception simultaneously and may produce a burst of re-fetch attempts on the next tick.

testing_gaps.description: No test covers fetch_ohlcv_async concurrent-caller dedup: specifically that when _fetch raises, all waiting coroutines receive the exception (not a hang) and subsequent calls correctly create a new future rather than awaiting the failed one.
No test verifies BatchQuickBacktestRequest rejects an oversized symbols list — because no such guard exists yet (see REL-2).
